justification:
Deduction hc 20988 references PROMO-2026-Q1-011 (Autumn In-Store Demo Program — Pita & Popcorn), an authorized demo-billback promotion covering SKUs MF-SN-001 and MF-SN-003 at $250/event during 2025-10-04 through 2025-11-15. The rate, SKUs, period, and promotion reference all check out, and the $7,500 claimed is within the $12,000 funding cap. No duplicate settlement was found.

However, the claim fails the documentation standard in contract section 3.4, which requires a signed Proof-of-Performance form (Exhibit B) for each demo event plus a store execution report, submitted within 30 days of the event. The retailer submitted neither; the remittance states "POP forms to follow," but by the Nov. 27 deduction date the 30-day submission window for the earliest October events has already closed.

Partial corroboration exists for 19 of the 30 claimed events: the attachments include store photos from 19 stores on various Oct/Nov weekends, and a Meridian field rep email stating "autumn demos ran well across the stores." These together substantiate 19 events. The remaining 11 events (30 – 19 = 11) have no supporting evidence beyond the retailer's own remittance text and cannot be paid.

Approved amount: 19 corroborated events × $250/event = $4,750.
Denied amount: 11 undocumented events × $250/event = $2,750 — rejected under contract section 3.4 for absence of Exhibit B and store execution reports.

The retailer may resubmit the denied 11 events if signed Exhibit B forms and execution reports can still be produced for those stores, subject to Meridian's review of their timeliness.
